name: Gil Goldstein
abstract: Gil Goldstein (born November 6, 1950, in Baltimore, Maryland) is an American jazz pianist and accordionist. He has won 5 Grammy Awards and he was nominated 8 times.

concerts.label: American Composers Orchestra: Sonidos de las Americas: Brasil
Rainforest Foundation Benefit Concert
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Malone
about_quotes.evidence: And it was Jon Faddis, Alex Foster, Bill Evans the tenor player, myself on the trombone, Danny Gottlieb, Mark Egan and Gil Goldstein. That was the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Varner
about_quotes.evidence: that was the one that was with Quincy and Miles at Montreux where they combined forces or the George Gruntz band and the Gil Evans, re-formed Gil Evans band with Gil Goldstein.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Varner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 13, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Don Alias
about_quotes.evidence: I wanted Danilo Perez on piano but for some reason he couldn’t make it so Gil Goldstein showed up... So now I’ve got a band with the Brecker brothers... and this guy
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Alias interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: And Gil and I were in the airport in Rome one time years ago... So I told you Gil, I think he has perfect pitch.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Diblasio
about_quotes.evidence: And this Gil Goldstein put out that great book The Jazz Composer, he interviews about twelve people in the back

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Gil Goldstein had sent him the parts in March or something like that, for this concert in the summer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019
